annotations.label: Polygon

anchor.snippet: a plane figure made up of line segments connected to form a closed polygonal chain

annotations.note: `Polygon P n` is a structure of `n` vertices in an affine space whose derived edges form a closed chain, generalizing the planar figure.

annotations.label: Edges and sides

anchor.snippet: The segments of a closed polygonal chain are called its edges or sides

annotations.note: `Polygon.edgeSet` defines each edge as the affine segment between consecutive vertices.

annotations.label: Vertices and corners

anchor.snippet: The points where two edges meet are the polygon's vertices or corners

annotations.note: `Polygon.vertices : Fin n → P` is exactly the indexed family of corners.

annotations.label: n-gon

anchor.snippet: An n -gon is a polygon with n sides

annotations.note: `Polygon P n` is parameterized by the number of vertices/edges `n`, directly capturing the n-gon.

annotations.label: Simple polygon

anchor.snippet: A simple polygon is one which does not intersect itself

annotations.note: Mathlib's `HasNondegenerateEdges`/`HasNondegenerateVertices` only ensure local nondegeneracy, not global non-self-intersection.

annotations.label: Solid polygon

anchor.snippet: A simple polygon is the boundary of a region of the plane that is called a solid polygon

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Polygon.boundary` but no notion of the filled interior region (solid polygon).

annotations.label: Star and self-intersecting polygons

anchor.snippet: A polygonal chain may cross over itself, creating star polygons

annotations.note: No definition of self-intersecting or star polygons exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polygon as polytope

anchor.snippet: A polygon is a 2-dimensional example of the more general polytope

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general geometric polytope structure of which polygons are the 2-dimensional case.

annotations.label: Convex polygon

anchor.snippet: any line drawn through the polygon

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Convex` for sets but not a dedicated convex-polygon predicate.

annotations.label: Non-convex polygon

anchor.snippet: a line may be found which meets its boundary more than twice

annotations.note: No non-convex polygon notion is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simple polygon (convexity)

anchor.snippet: the boundary of the polygon does not cross itself

annotations.note: Mathlib has no predicate for a polygon boundary not crossing itself.

annotations.label: Concave polygon

anchor.snippet: There is at least one interior angle greater than 180°

annotations.note: No concave polygon definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Star-shaped polygon

anchor.snippet: the whole interior is visible from at least one point

annotations.note: `StarConvex` formalizes the star-shaped property for general sets, of which a star-shaped polygon's interior would be an instance.

annotations.label: Self-intersecting polygon

anchor.snippet: the boundary of the polygon crosses itself

annotations.note: Mathlib has no predicate describing a self-intersecting polygon boundary.

annotations.label: Star polygon

anchor.snippet: a polygon which self-intersects in a regular way

annotations.note: No star polygon definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Star vs star-shaped

anchor.snippet: A polygon cannot be both a star and star-shaped

annotations.note: Neither star nor star-shaped polygons are defined, so this proposition is absent.

annotations.label: Equiangular polygon

anchor.snippet: all corner angles are equal

annotations.note: Mathlib has no equiangular polygon definition.

annotations.label: Equilateral polygon

anchor.snippet: all edges are of the same length

annotations.note: Mathlib has no equilateral polygon definition.

annotations.label: Regular polygon

anchor.snippet: both equilateral and equiangular

annotations.note: No regular polygon definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cyclic polygon

anchor.snippet: all corners lie on a single circle

annotations.note: `Concyclic` (cospherical and coplanar points) captures vertices lying on a common circle, but is stated for point sets, not polygons.

annotations.label: Tangential polygon

anchor.snippet: all sides are tangent to an inscribed circle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no tangential polygon definition.

annotations.label: Isogonal polygon

anchor.snippet: all corners lie within the same symmetry orbit

annotations.note: No isogonal polygon notion is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isotoxal polygon

anchor.snippet: all sides lie within the same symmetry orbit

annotations.note: No isotoxal polygon notion is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Characterizations of regularity

anchor.snippet: a polygon is regular if and only if it is both isogonal and isotoxal

annotations.note: The underlying regular/isogonal/isotoxal notions are absent, so this characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Regular star polygon

anchor.snippet: A non-convex regular polygon is called a regular star polygon

annotations.note: Mathlib has no regular star polygon definition.

annotations.label: Rectilinear polygon

anchor.snippet: the polygon's sides meet at right angles

annotations.note: No rectilinear polygon definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Monotone polygon

anchor.snippet: every line orthogonal to L intersects the polygon not more than twice

annotations.note: Mathlib has no monotone polygon definition.

annotations.label: Sum of interior angles

anchor.snippet: The sum of the interior angles of a simple n -gon is

annotations.note: Only the n=3 case (triangle angle sum = π) is proved; the general (n-2)·180° formula is absent.

annotations.label: Interior angle of convex regular n-gon

anchor.snippet: The measure of any interior angle of a convex regular n -gon is

annotations.note: No regular polygon is defined, so its interior-angle formula is absent.

annotations.label: Interior angle of regular star polygon

anchor.snippet: The interior angles of regular star polygons were first studied by Poinsot

annotations.note: Regular star polygons and their angle formulas are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Exterior angle

anchor.snippet: The exterior angle is the supplementary angle to the interior angle

annotations.note: Mathlib has angle notions but no exterior angle of a polygon defined.

annotations.label: Sum of exterior angles

anchor.snippet: the sum of the exterior angles must be 360°

annotations.note: The total turning / sum of exterior angles theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shoelace formula (signed area)

anchor.snippet: If the polygon is non-self-intersecting

annotations.note: The shoelace (signed area) formula is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lopshits area formula

anchor.snippet: The area A of a simple polygon can also be computed if the lengths of the sides

annotations.note: The Lopshits area formula is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pick's theorem

anchor.snippet: Pick's theorem gives a simple formula for the polygon's area

annotations.note: Pick's theorem is not in Mathlib (matches for 'Pick' are unrelated comments).

annotations.label: Isoperimetric inequality

anchor.snippet: In every polygon with perimeter p and area A

annotations.note: No isoperimetric inequality (for polygons or otherwise) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bolyai–Gerwien theorem

anchor.snippet: the Bolyai–Gerwien theorem asserts that the first can be cut into polygonal pieces

annotations.note: The Bolyai–Gerwien scissors-congruence theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cyclic polygon area determined by sides

anchor.snippet: if the polygon is simple and cyclic then the sides do determine the area

annotations.note: This result about cyclic polygon area is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maximal-area polygons are cyclic/regular

anchor.snippet: Of all n -gons with given side lengths, the one with the largest area is cyclic

annotations.note: This extremal-area characterization is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area of regular polygon (apothem)

anchor.snippet: The area of a regular polygon is given in terms of the radius r of its inscribed circle

annotations.note: No regular polygon area formula exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area of regular n-gon (circumradius)

anchor.snippet: The area of a regular n -gon can be expressed in terms of the radius R of its circumscribed circle

annotations.note: No circumradius-based regular polygon area formula exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area of self-intersecting polygon

anchor.snippet: The area of a self-intersecting polygon can be defined in two different ways

annotations.note: Self-intersecting polygons and their area conventions are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centroid of solid simple polygon

anchor.snippet: the coordinates of the centroid of a solid simple polygon are

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of the area centroid of a solid polygon region.

annotations.label: Centroid coincidence for triangles

anchor.snippet: the centroids of the vertices and of the solid shape are the same

annotations.note: The solid (area) centroid is not defined in Mathlib, so its coincidence with the vertex centroid cannot be stated.

annotations.label: Centroid of vertex set

anchor.snippet: The centroid of the vertex set of a polygon with n vertices has the coordinates

annotations.note: `Finset.centroid` is the equal-weight affine combination (average) of a finite point family, which is exactly the vertex-set centroid.

annotations.label: Spherical polygon

anchor.snippet: A spherical polygon is a circuit of arcs of great circles

annotations.note: Mathlib has no spherical polygon definition.

annotations.label: Digon

anchor.snippet: the digon , a polygon having only two sides and two corners

annotations.note: `Polygon P 2` is structurally the two-vertex case, though Mathlib does not capture the digon's degenerate/spherical geometry.

annotations.label: Skew polygon

anchor.snippet: A skew polygon does not lie in a flat plane

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Polygon` lives in arbitrary affine space but has no predicate distinguishing planar from skew.

annotations.label: Apeirogon

anchor.snippet: An apeirogon is an infinite sequence of sides and angles

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Polygon` requires a finite `n`, and no infinite apeirogon is defined.

annotations.label: Skew apeirogon

anchor.snippet: A skew apeirogon is an infinite sequence of sides and angles that do not lie in a flat plane

annotations.note: No skew apeirogon definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polygon with holes

anchor.snippet: A polygon with holes is an area-connected or multiply-connected planar polygon

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of a polygon with holes.

annotations.label: Complex polygon

anchor.snippet: A complex polygon is a configuration analogous to an ordinary polygon

annotations.note: No complex polygon configuration is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abstract polygon

anchor.snippet: An abstract polygon is an algebraic partially ordered set

annotations.note: Mathlib has graded-poset infrastructure (`Order.Grade`) but no abstract polytope/polygon definition.

annotations.label: Polyhedron

anchor.snippet: A polyhedron is a three-dimensional solid bounded by flat polygonal faces

annotations.note: Mathlib has no geometric polyhedron structure.

annotations.label: Polygon mesh

anchor.snippet: Any surface is modelled as a tessellation called polygon mesh

annotations.note: Polygon meshes are a computer-graphics concept not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Point in polygon test

anchor.snippet: This is called the point in polygon test

annotations.note: No point-in-polygon test or algorithm is formalized in Mathlib.
